role.name: Civil Engineer
role.description: Balance deadlines, materials, safety, and real-world load.

chapters.title: Bridge of Overconfidence
chapters.story: Mayor wants a bridge before the public event. Budget is low, deadline is close, and expectations are higher than the bridge itself.
chapters.task: Decide how to proceed with bridge planning.

outcome.title: Trampoline Bridge Ending
outcome.description: Bridge starts bouncing. Public loves it, safety department does not.
outcome.moralLesson: Safety cannot be sacrificed for speed or cost.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Safety cannot be sacrificed for speed or cost.

choices.text: Check load capacity, soil, material, and safety standards

outcome.title: Safe Structure Ending
outcome.description: You ask for proper calculations and review. The bridge stands. The mayor gets ribbon-cutting photos, not FIR papers.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.

choices.text: Make it rainbow colored and hope for the best

outcome.title: Instagram Disaster Ending
outcome.description: Bridge looks viral, but the safety report says “bro, seriously?”
outcome.moralLesson: Design appeal cannot replace structural safety.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Design appeal cannot replace structural safety.

miniGame.title: Arrange the Safe Bridge Workflow
miniGame.instructions: Put the bridge planning steps in the safest order.
miniGame.prompt: The client wants speed, but gravity has not approved shortcuts.
miniGame.hint: Survey first, then design checks, then approval, then execution.

orderItems.text: Start work because everyone is excited
Complete site survey and soil checks
Review structural design and load calculations
Get safety approval and execution plan sign-off

miniGame.successMessage: Solid workflow. The bridge is already standing confidently in the spreadsheet.
miniGame.failureMessage: The workflow skipped safety. The bridge drawing quietly turned into a trampoline design.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice safety walkthrough workflow for a Civil Engineer situation involving safety, load, materials.
safetyGuardrails: Do not approve unsafe work without inspection and documentation.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the safety walkthrough workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Safety-first simulation: unsafe site/material risks must be inspected, documented, and escalated before sign-off. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Site Safety Lead says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the safety walkthrough workflow before choosing speed.
chapters.safetyDisclaimer: Safety-first simulation: unsafe site/material risks must be inspected, documented, and escalated before sign-off.

guardrails: Do not approve unsafe work without inspection and documentation.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: Rainy Site Visit
chapters.story: Heavy rain hits the site. Waterlogging appears, workers are rushing, and the contractor says, “Sir, kaam chalne do.”
chapters.task: Decide how to manage site safety.

outcome.title: Mud Slide Ending
outcome.description: Materials float, boots disappear, and the site starts behaving like a water park.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.

choices.text: Inspect drainage, electrical risk, material storage, and worker safety

outcome.title: Safe Site Ending
outcome.description: You pause risky work, secure materials, and prevent a dangerous incident.
outcome.moralLesson: Site progress is useless without safety.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Site progress is useless without safety.

choices.text: Only take photos for WhatsApp update

outcome.title: Photo Engineer Ending
outcome.description: The group gets 17 photos and zero risk control. The site remains wet and sarcastic.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.

chapters.title: Concrete Countdown
chapters.story: Concrete pour is scheduled. Everyone wants speed, but testing and curing look like “extra formality” to the contractor.

outcome.title: Crack Festival Ending
outcome.description: Cracks arrive earlier than project payment. Contractor says it is “design texture.”

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.

choices.text: Verify mix, slump, placement, compaction, samples, and curing plan

outcome.title: Quality Pour Ending
outcome.description: The pour is controlled, records are clear, and the structure gets strength instead of excuses.
outcome.moralLesson: Concrete quality depends on process from start to curing.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Concrete quality depends on process from start to curing.

outcome.title: Remote Control Ending
outcome.description: You are present but not supervising. The site starts calling the contractor “boss.”

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Unsafe site decisions are never just comedy; inspection, documentation, and escalation come before sign-off.
